articles.title: A Unified Multimodal Framework for Dataset Construction and Model-Based Diagnosis of Ameloblastoma
articles.authors: George AB; John AM; Anoop A; Bhasuran B
articles.keywords: Ameloblastoma; Multimodal dataset; Surgical decision support; Clinical AI systems; NLP
articles.abstract: AI-enabled diagnostics in maxillofacial pathology require structured, high-quality multimodal datasets. A newly curated multimodal dataset focused on ameloblastoma integrates annotated radiological, histopathological, and intraoral clinical images with structured data from case reports. NLP was used to extract clinically relevant features; image data underwent domain-specific preprocessing and augmentation. A multimodal deep learning model was developed to classify ameloblastoma variants, assess recurrence risk, and support surgical planning. Variant classification accuracy improved from 46.2% to 65.9%; abnormal tissue detection F1-score improved from 43.0% to 90.3%. Benchmarked against MultiCaRe, this work advances patient-specific AI for maxillofacial pathology.
